assertions.assertion: Code uses ISessionManagementService with QuerySessionsAsync and CountRequested = 10 for the first page
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md shows exact code: `QuerySessionsAsync(new SessionQuery { CountRequested = 10 })`.

assertions.assertion: Code uses ResultsToken from the first query result to fetch the next page
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md explicitly demonstrates paging via `ResultsToken = userSessions.ResultsToken` in the next page query.

assertions.assertion: Code uses RemoveSessionsAsync with RevokeConsents = false to preserve consent grants
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md shows `RemoveSessionsAsync(new RemoveSessionsContext { ... RevokeConsents = false })` pattern.

assertions.assertion: Code sets SendBackchannelLogoutNotification = true (explicitly or by relying on the default) in RemoveSessionsContext
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md documents default of `SendBackchannelLogoutNotification` is true and shows how to set it explicitly.

assertions.assertion: Response mentions preferring GetSessionsAsync over QuerySessionsAsync for simple session listing
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md has a dedicated 'Performance Note on Querying' section stating to prefer GetSessionsAsync over QuerySessionsAsync.
